behaviors.description: Out-of-stock products are excluded even if they have high relevance scores.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at module handoff (inventory filter + ranker + result assembler), not just a single pure function. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE (Unit): a unit test for `isInStock()` passes, but pipeline code ranks first and only filters paginated slice, letting out-of-stock items leak. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no real OS/network/subprocess needed; no AI/ML output-quality dependency; no multi-agentic chain. REJECTION-GATE: disqualifies System/Agentic/Workflow because behavior text is deterministic rule filtering, with no real infra or model-judgment requirement.
behaviors.test_description: Wire recommendation service with real in-process catalog + inventory + ranking modules, mark a top-scoring item out-of-stock, assert it never appears in returned recommendations.

behaviors.description: Blocked user categories are excluded from recommendations.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: bug appears when user-settings data is handed to filtering stage. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE (Unit): category predicate unit test passes, but settings mapper uses wrong field name so blocked categories are not applied. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no real OS/network/subprocess; no model-output dependency; no multi-step agent chain. REJECTION-GATE: higher levels disqualified by behavior text focusing on explicit user settings filter logic.
behaviors.test_description: Run service with real settings repository adapter (in-memory/local DB) plus filter module; configure blocked categories and assert none appear in final ranked/paginated output.

behaviors.description: Items matching recent browsing signals rank above items with no browsing signal.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure often occurs in feature aggregation -> scoring -> sorting interaction. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE (Unit): scoring function test passes for fixed vectors, but browsing-event featurizer drops recency weights so ranking no longer prioritizes browsed categories. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no explicit real OS/network/subprocess requirement. LLM-DEP check: behavior does not state real external AI model call; treated as deterministic internal algorithm unless specified. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic rejected because behavior text references algorithm weighting signals, not external non-deterministic model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Build an end-to-end in-process ranking test with realistic user browse history and mixed candidate products; assert at least one browsing-aligned item outranks no-signal items under identical availability/preferences constraints.

behaviors.description: Pagination is correct and stable: requesting page 2 twice returns identical ordering when data is unchanged.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: instability appears when sorter + paginator + repository ordering interact. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE (Unit): paginator unit test passes on pre-sorted array, but repository returns ties without deterministic secondary key, causing page 2 drift across calls. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no real OS/network/subprocess required; no AI/ML output dependency; no multi-agentic steps. REJECTION-GATE: System disqualified because real external infra is not required to reproduce ordering bug.
behaviors.test_description: Use real in-process query/sort/pagination path over fixture data with tie scores; call page 2 twice without mutations and assert same IDs in same order.

behaviors.description: Results include at least one item from an unseen category (serendipity) to avoid filter bubble.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is usually in interaction between relevance ranker and diversification/re-ranking rule. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE (Unit): diversification helper unit test passes, but pipeline applies strict top-N cutoff before diversification so unseen categories never appear. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no explicit real OS/network/subprocess need. LLM-DEP: unless implemented via external model call, this is a deterministic business constraint over ranking output. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic rejected because behavior text specifies a policy requirement, not model judgment quality from a real AI API.
behaviors.test_description: Execute full in-process recommendation pipeline with user history concentrated in known categories; assert returned page contains at least one eligible item from an unseen category while respecting stock/preferences rules.

behaviors.description: Cold-start users receive popularity-based recommendations instead of empty results.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure occurs at boundary between user-history lookup and fallback strategy selection. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE (Unit): popularity scorer unit test passes, but orchestrator treats missing history as error and returns empty list before fallback path runs. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no real OS/network/subprocess required; no AI/ML output quality requirement; no multi-agentic sequence. REJECTION-GATE: System/Agentic/Workflow disqualified by deterministic fallback behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Run recommendation request for a user with no events in real in-process modules (history store + fallback ranker); assert non-empty list and that ordering follows popularity signal.

behaviors.description: Click tracking stores correct product ID, rank position, and timestamp per interaction.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failures appear when UI/event payload, recommendation list context, and tracking persistence are wired together. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE (Unit): event parser unit test passes, but integration loses list position after pagination transform, storing wrong rank. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: can be validated with in-process store and injectable clock; no mandatory real OS/network/subprocess. No AI/ML dependency; no multi-agentic chain. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because behavior text does not require real external transport or OS-level timing primitives.
behaviors.test_description: Generate recommendation list, emit click event through real tracking handler stack, then assert persisted record contains exact product ID, displayed position, and expected timestamp.

behaviors.description: Recommendation generation stays under 200ms for a 100,000-product catalog.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: latency behavior depends on real process/runtime, memory, and I/O characteristics; mocks hide true performance. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE (Unit): optimized scoring function unit passes microbench, but full process exceeds 200ms due to serialization/allocation overhead. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE (Integration): in-process mocked repository passes quickly, but real service process with actual data loading/index access breaches SLA. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic/Workflow disqualified because no model-judgment or multi-agent pipeline is described.
behaviors.test_description: Run a production-like service instance and execute recommendation requests against a real 100k-product dataset, measuring end-to-end latency percentile and asserting threshold <200ms under defined load profile.

behaviors.description: After purchase, previously recommended product is deprioritized in subsequent recommendations.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: bug is in cross-module state handoff (purchase event ingestion -> profile update -> ranking weights). ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE (Unit): rank-penalty function unit test passes, but purchase event processor writes wrong product key so penalty never applies. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no real OS/network/subprocess required; no required AI/ML non-deterministic output; no multi-agentic sequence. REJECTION-GATE: System/Agentic/Workflow rejected since behavior text describes deterministic state update and re-ranking.
behaviors.test_description: Execute two requests with a real in-process event/update pipeline: generate recommendations, record purchase for one recommended item, regenerate and assert purchased item rank drops relative to baseline.

behaviors.description: For unchanged user state, repeated recommendation requests return identical ranked lists.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: nondeterminism often emerges from module interactions (unordered data retrieval, tie-breaking, seed handling) rather than one function. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE (Unit): sorter unit test with fixed input passes, but repository iteration order varies and no stable tie-break key causes rank drift between calls. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no mandatory real OS/network/subprocess; no real AI/ML judgment dependency stated; no multi-agentic chain. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because identical-state determinism can be validated in-process without external infra.
behaviors.test_description: Run full in-process recommendation pipeline twice for same user snapshot with no intervening writes; assert exact ID sequence equality across outputs.
